prompt:
You are the SCAFFOLDER (opus) in an autonomous substrate-build loop. You build a generic, reusable Lean substrate module.

## Requirement
# Substrate requirement: l2_residual_quadratic_projection

## Goal
Closed-form L¬≤ projection residual of `y ‚Ü¶ y¬≤` onto `span{1, y}` in `L¬≤(őľ)` for a probability measure `őľ` on `‚ĄĚ` with finite fourth moment (the residual variance of regressing `y¬≤` on `(1, y)`).

## Provides (API contract)
- `residualQuad (őľ) (b‚āÄ b‚āĀ : ‚ĄĚ) : ‚ĄĚ := ‚ąę y, (y^2 - b‚āÄ - b‚āĀ * y)^2 ‚ąāőľ` ‚ÄĒ the quadratic objective.
- `l2ResidualQuadratic (őľ) : ‚ĄĚ` ‚ÄĒ the closed-form minimum residual `r(őľ) = (m‚āĄ ‚ąí m‚āā¬≤) ‚ąí (m‚āÉ ‚ąí m‚āĀ m‚āā)¬≤ / (m‚āā ‚ąí m‚āĀ¬≤)` in the moments `m‚āĖ = ‚ąę yŠĶŹ ‚ąāőľ` (equivalently the Hankel-determinant ratio `det H‚āÉ / det H‚āā`).
- `optIntercept (őľ) : ‚ĄĚ`, `optSlope (őľ) : ‚ĄĚ` ‚ÄĒ the least-squares optimal coefficients `b‚āĀ* = (m‚āÉ ‚ąí m‚āĀ m‚āā)/(m‚āā ‚ąí m‚āĀ¬≤)`, `b‚āÄ* = m‚āā ‚ąí b‚āĀ* m‚āĀ`.
- `residualQuad_opt_eq : m‚āĀ¬≤ < m‚āā ‚Üí residualQuad őľ (optIntercept őľ) (optSlope őľ) = l2ResidualQuadratic őľ` (attainment).
- `l2ResidualQuadratic_le : m‚āĀ¬≤ < m‚āā ‚Üí l2ResidualQuadratic őľ ‚Č§ residualQuad őľ b‚āÄ b‚āĀ` (it is the infimum ‚ÄĒ normal-equation / SOS certificate).
- `l2ResidualQuadratic_nonneg : 0 ‚Č§ l2ResidualQuadratic őľ`.

## Statement / milestones
For a probability measure `őľ` on `‚ĄĚ` with finite 4th moment and non-degenerate `Var(y) = m‚āā ‚ąí m‚āĀ¬≤ > 0`:
`‚®Ö (b‚āÄ b‚āĀ : ‚ĄĚ), residualQuad őľ b‚āÄ b‚āĀ = l2ResidualQuadratic őľ`, attained at `(optIntercept őľ, optSlope őľ)`.
Prove via the normal equations for least squares of `y¬≤` on `{1, y}`: expand `residualQuad őľ b‚āÄ b‚āĀ` as a positive-semidefinite quadratic in `(b‚āÄ, b‚āĀ)`, whose minimizer is the normal-equation solution and whose minimum value is the stated Hankel ratio. The `‚Č§` (infimum) direction is a completed-square / SOS identity; attainment is a direct `field_simp; ring` at the optimum.

## Standard reference
Standard least-squares / Hankel-moment-matrix algebra; e.g. any regression or moment-problem text (Shohat‚ÄďTamarkin, *The Problem of Moments*; or the finite-dimensional orthogonal-projection / Gram-matrix residual formula).

## Intended reuse
Consumed by the thmsmith run `stat_neyman_regret_minimax`: `armTangentStrength őĹ a = ‚®Ö b‚āÄ b‚āĀ, ‚ąę (y¬≤ ‚ąí b‚āÄ ‚ąí b‚āĀ y)¬≤ d(armMarginal őĹ a)` and `arm_score_program_solution` (the moment-preserving score program value `J_{a,őĹ}(x) = x¬≤ / r_{a,őĹ}`). Required at general-measure generality (arm marginals on `[0,1]`), not gerrymandered to that setting.

## May assume / must derive
May assume: `őľ` is a probability (or finite) measure with `‚ąę y‚Āī ‚ąāőľ < ‚ąě` (all lower moments finite), and non-degeneracy `m‚āĀ¬≤ < m‚āā`. Must derive: the closed-form residual, optimality, attainment, and non-negativity ‚ÄĒ from the integral definition (no assuming the Hankel formula).

## Known building blocks
`MeasureTheory.integral_*` (linearity, `integral_add`, `integral_smul`, `integral_const`), `inner_mul_le_norm_mul_norm` / completed square, and `Causalean.Stat.Nonparametric.MomentEnvelope` (`momentResidual`, its SOS min-certificate) for the moment-level algebra ‚ÄĒ the new content is the MEASURE-level `‚ąę`-form and the `‚®Ö`/optimality bridge.

## Target module
Causalean.Stat.Nonparametric.L2ResidualQuadratic


## Workspace
- Write/extend Lean files under: /<repo-root>/CausalSmith/CausalSmith/Substrate/L2ResidualQuadraticProjection
- Module prefix (one file per module): CausalSmith.Substrate.L2ResidualQuadraticProjection.<Name>
- This is build round 1 of at most 10.

## Your task this turn
0. ASSESS CURRENT STATUS FROM GROUND TRUTH. You are a fresh invocation with NO memory of prior turns ‚ÄĒ your only context is this prompt plus what you read from disk. Read the current files under the Lean dir and check their real state (remaining `sorry`s and any errors) via lean-lsp diagnostics or a targeted build. Trust the files over the prior round report (a report can lag a partial filler edit). The report and your prior plan below tell you the INTENT; the files tell you the TRUTH.
1. Search the existing library FIRST (run `npm run search -- "<concept>"` in CausalSmith/tools; use lean-lsp local/leansearch) to reuse, not reinvent.
1b. FETCH THE PRIMARY SOURCE when possible. If the Requirement names a reference (or you find the canonical one), fetch it to ground the exact statement, hypotheses, and naming/conventions you formalize ‚ÄĒ via WebFetch/WebSearch or Bash `curl`/`wget`; for an arXiv paper download the LaTeX source and read the `.tex`, not the PDF. Skip only when no source is named or none is reachable; never block on it.
2. UPDATE `plan_markdown` as a STRUCTURED STATUS LEDGER (this IS the memory the next turn inherits ‚ÄĒ make it concrete, not a vision doc). Use these sections: `## Done` (decls proven/closed), `## Remaining` (the open sorries, by file), `## Blocked` (what's stuck and why), `## Decisions` (design/API choices + what was tried and rejected, so the next turn doesn't repeat dead ends). Keep it CONCISE ‚ÄĒ bullet points referencing decl/file names, NOT full proof derivations. Per-lemma proof strategy and tactic sketches belong in the Lean file as docstrings/comments (you write those to disk directly), not duplicated into this JSON string.
3. WRITE or EXTEND the Lean scaffold: precise statements with `sorry` for unproven proofs. The file(s) MUST compile with only `sorry` warnings (no hard errors). Keep each file focused (<300 lines) with a `/-! -/` module docstring and a docstring on each public declaration (first paragraph = self-contained NL translation).
4. DECIDE how many codex subagents to use to fill the sorries this round, and emit ONE prompt per subagent (partition the sorries as you judge best).

## Integrity (HARD RULES ‚ÄĒ non-negotiable)
- Do NOT LAUNDER. Never weaken, narrow, or special-case a statement, and never delete or relax a hypothesis that should be proven, just to make the build pass or satisfy the reviewer. The statements must stay the genuine, general results the Requirement asks for. Strengthening an assumption to dodge a hard case, replacing the real goal with a corollary, or hiding the difficulty in a definition all count as laundering ‚ÄĒ do not do it.
- Do NOT create VACUOUS statements. No trivially-true claims, no `True`-like lemmas, no uninhabitable/contradictory hypotheses, no premises that are never satisfiable (an always-false hypothesis makes any conclusion hold for free). Every theorem must have real content and be applicable to the intended inhabited inputs.
- When revising after a failed review, FIX THE PROOF / API ‚ÄĒ not the goalposts. Address the findings without diluting what the module proves.
- If a clean, true, non-vacuous statement is genuinely out of reach, choose decision="escalate" with the reason. Escalating is correct; laundering or shipping a vacuous statement is a failure.

## Decisions
- decision="build": you wrote/updated the scaffold and want the fillers to run this round. Provide `codex_prompts` (>=1).
- decision="review": choose this ONLY after you have VERIFIED from ground truth that the module compiles with ZERO remaining sorries and zero errors (confirm via diagnostics/build this turn ‚ÄĒ do not infer it from the report) AND the statements are the genuine, non-vacuous results (no laundering). `codex_prompts` may be empty.
- decision="escalate": the goal is wrong, unrealistic, or only reachable by weakening it into something vacuous; set `escalation.reason`. The run halts.

Emit ONLY the StructuredOutput JSON object: { decision, plan_markdown, codex_prompts: [{id, target_decls, prompt}], escalation? }.
`plan_markdown` is your living status ledger (persisted to plan.md and fed back to you next turn) ‚ÄĒ keep it accurate.
KEEP THE WHOLE EMITTED JSON COMPACT (target: a few KB, not tens of KB). Detailed proof strategy, Lean snippets, and long derivations go in the Lean files on disk (docstrings/comments) and in FOCUSED per-sorry `codex_prompts` ‚ÄĒ never as a bulk dump inside `plan_markdown`. A very large structured emit can corrupt the tool-call encoding, so a bounded emit is a correctness requirement, not just style.
rawOutput:
The target directory is fresh (round 1). I have the moment-level algebra in `Causalean.Stat.Nonparametric.MomentEnvelope` to reuse. Let me write the measure-level scaffold.
Now let me verify the scaffold compiles with only sorry warnings (no hard errors):
No hard errors ‚ÄĒ the three downstream proofs compiled. Two sorries remain (bridge at line 94, iInf at line 139). Let me tidy the long-line warnings.
Scaffold compiles with only the two intended `sorry` warnings. Now I'll emit the build decision with two focused filler prompts.

  are new. Verified momentResidual = target Hankel form (m‚āĄ‚ąím‚āā¬≤)‚ąí(m‚āÉ‚ąím‚āĀm‚āā)¬≤/(m‚āā‚ąím‚āĀ¬≤).
- `FiniteMoment4` as integrability of the 4 powers (requirement explicitly allows assuming lower
  moments finite). Non-degeneracy `m‚āĀ¬≤<m‚āā` carried on opt_eq/le/nonneg/iInf (honest domain, matches
  MomentEnvelope hyps) ‚ÄĒ NOT laundering.

Do NOT change any statement/signature or touch other declarations. The module compiles now with only sorry warnings; keep it that way (only sorry warnings must remain, none for this decl).

Goal: for `őľ : Measure ‚ĄĚ`, `[IsProbabilityMeasure őľ]`, `h : FiniteMoment4 őľ`, `b‚āÄ b‚āĀ : ‚ĄĚ`, prove
`residualQuad őľ b‚āÄ b‚āĀ = Causalean.Stat.MomentEnvelope.residualQuad (moment őľ 1) (moment őľ 2) (moment őľ 3) (moment őľ 4) b‚āÄ b‚āĀ`.
Defs: `residualQuad őľ b‚āÄ b‚āĀ = ‚ąę y, (y^2 - b‚āÄ - b‚āĀ*y)^2 ‚ąāőľ`; `moment őľ k = ‚ąę y, y^k ‚ąāőľ`; MomentEnvelope.residualQuad m1 m2 m3 m4 b0 b1 = `m4 - 2*b1*m3 - 2*b0*m2 + b1^2*m2 + 2*b0*b1*m1 + b0^2`. `FiniteMoment4` fields: `h.int1 : Integrable (fun y:‚ĄĚ=>y) őľ`, `h.int2 : Integrable (fun y:‚ĄĚ=>y^2) őľ`, `h.int3 : Integrable (fun y=>y^3) őľ`, `h.int4 : Integrable (fun y=>y^4) őľ`.

Strategy: rewrite the integrand pointwise (via `simp only`/`congr`+`funext`+`ring` or a `have : (fun y => (y^2-b‚āÄ-b‚āĀ*y)^2) = fun y => y^4 - 2*b‚āĀ*y^3 + (b‚āĀ^2 - 2*b‚āÄ)*y^2 + 2*b‚āÄ*b‚āĀ*y + b‚āÄ^2`) then split the integral. Split with `MeasureTheory.integral_add`/`integral_sub` (each needs the two parts Integrable) and pull scalars with `MeasureTheory.integral_const_mul`; the constant term is `MeasureTheory.integral_const` giving `(őľ Set.univ).toReal ‚ÄĘ b‚āÄ^2`, and `measure_univ`/`IsProbabilityMeasure` makes it `b‚āÄ^2` (simp with `measure_univ`, `ENNReal.one_toReal`, `smul_eq_mul`, `one_mul`). Build integrability of each piece from `h.int1..int4`, `Integrable.const_mul`, `Integrable.add`, `Integrable.sub`, `integrable_const`. Reconcile `moment őľ 1 = ‚ąę y^1` with `‚ąę y` via `pow_one` (e.g. `simp only [pow_one]` or note moment őľ 1 unfolds to ‚ąę y^1). Unfold `residualQuad`, `moment`, `Causalean.Stat.MomentEnvelope.residualQuad` as needed. Finish with `ring` after all integrals are evaluated. Use lean-lsp `lean_goal`/`lean_diagnostic_messages` on this file to iterate. If term-by-term splitting is painful, an alternative: prove `Integrable (fun y => (y^2-b‚āÄ-b‚āĀ*y)^2) őľ` and use `integral_congr`/linearity lemmas; but the direct expand-and-split is expected to work. Verify final state has no errors for this decl."},{"id":"iinf","target_decls":["CausalSmith.Substrate.L2ResidualQuadratic.iInf_residualQuad"],"prompt":"Fill the single `sorry` in theorem `iInf_residualQuad` in file `/<repo-root>/CausalSmith/CausalSmith/Substrate/L2ResidualQuadraticProjection/Basic.lean`. Do NOT change any statement/signature or other declarations; keep only-sorry-warnings status (none for this decl).

Goal: `‚®Ö b‚āÄ, ‚®Ö b‚āĀ, residualQuad őľ b‚āÄ b‚āĀ = l2ResidualQuadratic őľ` given `[IsProbabilityMeasure őľ]`, `h : FiniteMoment4 őľ`, `hnd : moment őľ 1 ^ 2 < moment őľ 2`.

Available ALREADY-PROVEN lemmas in this file:
- `l2ResidualQuadratic_le őľ h hnd b‚āÄ b‚āĀ : l2ResidualQuadratic őľ ‚Č§ residualQuad őľ b‚āÄ b‚āĀ` (for all b‚āÄ b‚āĀ).
- `residualQuad_opt_eq őľ h hnd : residualQuad őľ (optIntercept őľ) (optSlope őľ) = l2ResidualQuadratic őľ`.

Use `le_antisymm`.
(‚Č•) `l2ResidualQuadratic őľ ‚Č§ ‚®Ö b‚āÄ, ‚®Ö b‚āĀ, residualQuad őľ b‚āÄ b‚āĀ`: `le_ciInf (fun b‚āÄ => le_ciInf (fun b‚āĀ => l2ResidualQuadratic_le őľ h hnd b‚āÄ b‚āĀ))`.
(‚Č§) `‚®Ö b‚āÄ, ‚®Ö b‚āĀ, residualQuad őľ b‚āÄ b‚āĀ ‚Č§ l2ResidualQuadratic őľ`: chain
`‚®Ö b‚āÄ, ‚®Ö b‚āĀ, residualQuad őľ b‚āÄ b‚āĀ ‚Č§ ‚®Ö b‚āĀ, residualQuad őľ (optIntercept őľ) b‚āĀ ‚Č§ residualQuad őľ (optIntercept őľ) (optSlope őľ) = l2ResidualQuadratic őľ`.
Each step is `ciInf_le` which needs a `BddBelow` witness for the range: the range of `fun b‚āĀ => residualQuad őľ b0 b‚āĀ` (resp. `fun b‚āÄ => ‚®Ö b‚āĀ, residualQuad őľ b‚āÄ b‚āĀ`) is bounded below by `l2ResidualQuadratic őľ` using `l2ResidualQuadratic_le`. Construct `BddBelow` via `‚ü®l2ResidualQuadratic őľ, fun x hx => ...‚ü©` with `Set.forall_mem_range`/`rintro`. For the outer one, note `l2ResidualQuadratic őľ ‚Č§ ‚®Ö b‚āĀ, residualQuad őľ b‚āÄ b‚āĀ` also holds by `le_ciInf`. Then finish with `residualQuad_opt_eq`.
Relevant Mathlib lemmas: `le_ciInf`, `ciInf_le`, `BddBelow`, `Set.mem_range`, `Real.instConditionallyCompleteLattice` (‚ĄĚ is conditionally complete). Iterate with lean-lsp `lean_goal`/`lean_diagnostic_messages` on this file until this decl has no errors and only sorry warnings remain elsewhere."}]}

## Status
Round 1. Fresh module created at `CausalSmith/Substrate/L2ResidualQuadraticProjection/Basic.lean`
(namespace `CausalSmith.Substrate.L2ResidualQuadratic`). Compiles: only 2 `sorry` warnings, no errors.

## Done
- `moment`, `FiniteMoment4` (int of y,y¬≤,y¬≥,y‚Āī), `residualQuad`, `l2ResidualQuadratic`, `optIntercept`,
  `optSlope` defined. Closed form delegated to `Causalean.Stat.MomentEnvelope` (moment-level algebra
  already proven there: `momentResidual`, `residualQuad_optimalCoeff`, `residualQuad_ge_momentResidual`).
- `residualQuad_opt_eq`, `l2ResidualQuadratic_le`, `l2ResidualQuadratic_nonneg` ‚ÄĒ PROVEN (each is a
  one-liner off the bridge `residualQuad_eq` + the MomentEnvelope lemmas; defeq of opt coeffs confirmed
  by successful compile).

## Remaining (sorries in Basic.lean)
- `residualQuad_eq` (line ~94): bridge ‚ąę(y¬≤‚ąíb‚āÄ‚ąíb‚āĀy)¬≤ = MomentEnvelope.residualQuad m‚āĀ m‚āā m‚āÉ m‚āĄ b‚āÄ b‚āĀ.
- `iInf_residualQuad` (line ~139): ‚®Ö b‚āÄ b‚āĀ, residualQuad = l2ResidualQuadratic.

## Decisions
- Reuse Causalean MomentEnvelope for ALL polynomial/SOS algebra; only the ‚ąę-expansion + ciInf bridge
  are new. Verified momentResidual = target Hankel form (m‚āĄ‚ąím‚āā¬≤)‚ąí(m‚āÉ‚ąím‚āĀm‚āā)¬≤/(m‚āā‚ąím‚āĀ¬≤).
- `FiniteMoment4` as integrability of the 4 powers (requirement explicitly allows assuming lower
  moments finite). Non-degeneracy `m‚āĀ¬≤<m‚āā` carried on opt_eq/le/nonneg/iInf (honest domain, matches
  MomentEnvelope hyps) ‚ÄĒ NOT laundering.
- `import Mathlib` (some sibling study files do the same) to avoid import-hunting for integral/ciInf.
